text.title: django-api Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for Django REST API surface.

rule.statement: Views contain no business logic. Body limited to: (1) serializer validation, (2) ONE service call, (3) Response construction. Body length <= 25 lines.
rule.rationale: Thin views keep the HTTP layer testable in isolation and route all writes through one place (services). Business logic in views resists testing and migration to async / different transport.

rule.statement: Service functions accept domain types (User instance, Decimal amount), NEVER `request.user`, `request.POST`, or raw dicts.
rule.rationale: Services should be callable from views, tasks, and management commands with identical signatures. Coupling them to HTTP request objects locks them to the view layer.

rule.statement: Use `drf-spectacular` for OpenAPI generation. Decorate each ViewSet/action with `@extend_schema`. Validate that `openapi.json` parses with `oasdiff` in CI.
rule.rationale: drf-spectacular keeps the schema in lockstep with code. Manual schema files drift the first sprint after they ship.

rule.statement: Use `ViewSet` (and `ModelViewSet` for CRUD) over flat `APIView` for resources following REST conventions. Stick to `APIView` for genuinely non-RESTful endpoints (custom RPC, webhooks).
rule.rationale: ViewSets generate consistent URL patterns and play well with `drf-spectacular`. APIView is escape hatch only.

rule.statement: Every list endpoint MUST be paginated. Use cursor pagination for large or append-only datasets; page-number pagination otherwise. Default `page_size = 20`, max `page_size = 100`.
rule.rationale: Unpaginated lists DoS production the first time a customer accumulates 10k rows. Page-number pagination is fine for small sets; cursor pagination is required for streams where rows shift.

rule.statement: Error responses follow RFC 7807 problem-detail shape: `{type, title, status, detail, instance}`. Wire via `drf-spectacular`'s exception handler integration.
rule.rationale: Consistent error shape makes consumer code uniform across endpoints. Mixed error shapes (DRF default vs custom JSON) cost consumer teams real hours.
